skill.name: alphafold-database
skill.description: Access AlphaFold DB protein structure predictions, download coordinate files, and interpret confidence metrics for structural biology research.
skill.summary: Retrieve AlphaFold structures and assess prediction confidence.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many severe patterns, but most are false positives from biological sequences, 3D-Beacons text, public URLs, and Markdown code fences. Confirmed concerns are documentation examples that use shell execution and a curl-to-bash installer pattern. No evidence found of malicious intent, credential access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Shell Execution in Proteome Download Example
medium_findings.description: The Python example builds a gsutil command from taxonomy_id and output_dir, then runs it with shell=True. If copied with untrusted input, this can allow command injection. Use argument arrays and validate taxonomy IDs as integers.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The finding is direct: subprocess.run uses shell=True with a formatted command. It is documentation, not active skill code, so exploitation depends on a user copying and adapting it.

medium_findings.title: Pipe to Shell Installation Pattern
medium_findings.description: The API reference recommends piping a downloaded Google Cloud SDK installer directly into bash. This executes remote code without an integrity check, even though the URL is a legitimate vendor endpoint.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicitly present as curl piped to bash. The endpoint is legitimate, so this is an unsafe copy-paste pattern rather than evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Public Network Access
low_findings.description: Network examples contact AlphaFold DB, UniProt, EBI 3D-Beacons, Google Cloud, and public documentation resources. These calls support the skill purpose and do not show credential transmission or covert destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The domains are public scientific or vendor resources named by the skill. I found no evidence of secret collection, unknown endpoints, or exfiltration logic.

low_findings.title: Static Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: Critical hits for Windows SAM, Cobalt Strike, weak cryptography, and device-file access are false positives. They come from protein sequence text, 3D-Beacons references, version labels, and a Biopython documentation URL.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows scientific data and documentation names, not malware tooling, credential stores, weak crypto use, or device access.

low_findings.title: Expected Local File Writes in Examples
low_findings.description: Examples write downloaded coordinate files and generated plots to local paths. This is expected for structure analysis, but users should avoid privileged or sensitive output paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The writes are visible and tied to downloaded research files or plots. The risk is low because there is no hidden overwrite logic, but copied examples can still affect local files.

dangerous_patterns.title: shell=True with Formatted Command
dangerous_patterns.description: A shell command is assembled with formatted values before execution. This pattern should be replaced with subprocess.run argument arrays and strict input validation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code shows shell=True on a string command containing variables. The risk is limited to users who copy the sample.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Installer Piped to Shell
dangerous_patterns.description: The installer command pipes a remote script directly into bash. Users should prefer package managers, checksums, or vendor-signed installers.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous pipe-to-shell pattern is explicit. The source is a legitimate Google endpoint, which lowers confidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Protein structure work often requires finding models, files, and confidence data across several services. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through AlphaFold DB retrieval, downloads, and interpretation workflows.
actual_capabilities: Find AlphaFold prediction metadata for UniProt accessions.
Download model files in mmCIF, PDB, and related AlphaFold formats.
Explain pLDDT confidence scores and PAE matrix interpretation.
Guide batch downloads through Google Cloud public datasets.
Show Biopython, requests, BigQuery, and gsutil workflow examples.
Support structural biology, drug discovery, and protein engineering research tasks.
limitations: It provides documentation and examples, not a hosted AlphaFold API client.
AlphaFold predictions are computational models, not experimental structures.
Bulk downloads can require significant bandwidth, storage, and Google Cloud setup.
Users must verify current database versions and service availability.

use_cases.title: Retrieve a Target Structure
use_cases.description: Find an AlphaFold model for a UniProt accession and download the coordinate file for analysis.

use_cases.title: Screen Model Confidence
use_cases.description: Review pLDDT and PAE values before using predicted structures in docking or binding-site studies.

use_cases.title: Build a Proteome Dataset
use_cases.description: Plan batch retrieval of organism-level AlphaFold predictions using Google Cloud storage and metadata queries.

prompt_templates.title: Find One Protein Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the alphafold-database skill to find the AlphaFold model for UniProt ID P00520. Explain the available file formats.

prompt_templates.title: Download and Check Confidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the alphafold-database skill to outline a Python workflow that downloads a model and summarizes pLDDT confidence regions.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Multiple Targets
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the alphafold-database skill to plan a batch comparison for P00520, P12931, and P04637 with average pLDDT results.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Proteome Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the alphafold-database skill to design a reproducible pipeline for downloading one species proteome and querying metadata in BigQuery.

output_examples.input: Find the AlphaFold entry for P00520.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the AlphaFold entry, available coordinate formats, confidence files, and the best next step for analysis.

output_examples.input: Explain whether a low pLDDT region is reliable for docking.
output_examples.output: The response explains pLDDT thresholds and recommends avoiding low-confidence regions for atom-level docking decisions.
The response describes Google Cloud storage access, local storage planning, version tracking, and rate-aware workflow choices.

best_practices: Validate UniProt accessions before downloading large files.
Check pLDDT and PAE before using any predicted structure downstream.
Cache downloaded files and record the AlphaFold database version.
anti_patterns: Treating AlphaFold predictions as experimental structures without confidence review.
Running bulk downloads without estimating storage, bandwidth, and costs.
Using shell commands with unvalidated identifiers or output paths.

faq.question: Does this skill run AlphaFold predictions?
faq.answer: No. It helps access existing AlphaFold DB predictions and related confidence data.

faq.question: Which identifier should I provide?
faq.answer: Use a UniProt accession when possible, such as P00520.

faq.question: Can it download PDB files?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation includes AlphaFold file URL patterns for PDB, mmCIF, BCIF, confidence, and PAE files.

faq.question: What do pLDDT scores mean?
faq.answer: pLDDT scores estimate per-residue confidence. Higher values are more reliable for detailed structural interpretation.

faq.question: Is it safe for bulk datasets?
faq.answer: Use caution. Bulk downloads can be large and should use validated commands, storage planning, and version tracking.

faq.question: Does it require authentication?
faq.answer: Most AlphaFold DB file and REST access is public. BigQuery and Google Cloud workflows may need local cloud configuration.
